narrative: OMX30 faces a mixed morning with fragmented global signals. DAX and CAC rallied overnight while S&P 500 and Dow fell 0.41–1.13%, creating near-zero correlation bias (−0.037%). Domestically, the index sits at 79% of 52-week range and 5.2% below highs despite +7.4% above 200-MA, signaling conflicting strength/weakness. Technically, weak ADX (15.9, well below 18) and MACD below signal demand confidence reduction by 30%. Copper's +2.92% surge and gold's +0.74% gain are bright spots for Boliden and mining stocks. USD/SEK at 9.287 (+0.82%) supports Volvo, Atlas Copco, and Ericsson, but sector rotation into banks (yields up +155bps on US 10Y) faces headwinds from stretched valuations and weak momentum.
morningBrief: OMX30 opens with minimal directional bias—expect range trading 3105–3150. DAX positive overnight (+1.21%) props upside while FTSE weakness (−1.06%) and US selloff temper enthusiasm. Key watch: Boliden on copper strength (+2.92%)—outperform play if commodity momentum holds. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) benefit from US 10Y yield jump to 4.446% but watch for profit-taking after recent runs. Exporters (Volvo B, Atlas Copco, Ericsson B) catch tailwind from USD/SEK +0.82%, but NASDAQ weakness (−0.19%) and weak ADX (15.9) suggest caution—trim long positions into strength. RSI at 37.08 signals mean-reversion buy setup if OMX dips to 3085 (lower Bollinger band). VIX compression (−3.28% to 17.69) reflects risk-on mood but fragile. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for directional cues.
drivers: Copper surge +2.92% supports industrials and mining
USD/SEK strength (+0.82%) benefits exporters but offset by weak technicals
Weak ADX (15.9) and MACD below signal reduce conviction
RSI 37.08 approaching oversold — mean reversion risk

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +155bps to 4.446% widens NIM expectations; Swedish rates likely follow.
USD/SEK +0.82% to 9.287 boosts export margins; copper +2.92% supports industrial input demand.
Copper +2.92% and gold +0.74% drive direct revenue; Boliden primary beneficiary.
NASDAQ −0.19% and weak global tech sentiment offset USD/SEK export gains; wait for clarity.
US 10Y yield +155bps raises refinancing costs and DCF discount rates.
Oil down −1.27% is modest tailwind, but EUR/SEK +0.45% raises H&M sourcing costs; macro uncertainty pressures discretionary.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.92% and gold +0.74% represent direct revenue multipliers; strongest upside catalyst in index.
USD/SEK +0.82% supports export margins; industrials +1.21% DAX tailwind; watch for profit-taking if ADX stays weak.
US 10Y +155bps widens NIM; but valuation risk if yields stabilize. Upside capped by weak ADX confidence.
Caught between USD/SEK export strength and NASDAQ weakness; needs clearer directional bias before entry.
EUR/SEK +0.45% raises sourcing costs; oil −1.27% insufficient offset; consumer sentiment remains pressured.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish economic releases. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (consensus 213k) — key test of labor market strength and yield persistence.
topRisk: Weak ADX (15.9) and MACD below signal indicate low-conviction technicals; any reversal in US yields or hawkish Fed messaging could trigger sharp downside retest of 3085 support. FTSE −1.06% and Hang Seng −0.76% suggest geopolitical/macro uncertainty lurking beneath surface. Trade war escalation would spike VIX and flush risk assets.
technicalSummary: Bull market regime but +7.4% above 200-MA / −0.0% vs 50-MA signals exhaustion
MACD below signal (hist −10.226) = bearish divergence despite higher highs
ADX 15.9 << 18 = weak trend, reduce all confidence by 30%
RSI 37.08 near oversold, mean-reversion buy setup on dips
Bollinger %B 0.31 (lower half) = squeeze/compression phase; width 6.29% tight
ROC-90 +9.3% shows strong 90-day momentum but at risk of mean reversion
Stochastic K=29.2, D=26.1 = neutral, no overbought/oversold signal yet
